title: phoradendron californicum var. leucocarpum
intro: Phoradendron californicum var. leucocarpum, also known as California mistletoe or white-berried mistletoe, is a plant species that is a type of parasitic shrub native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and swelling, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Phoradendron californicum var. leucocarpum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make capsules or other medicinal products.
